title: Spar
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Youmay|the [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|ARC trooper]] and [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]]|the [[Spar sector]] or [[Alpha (disambiguation)|other uses of the term Alpha]]|linebreak=1}}

{{Quote|Why take prisoners? Why not just shoot them?|Spar|<ref name="O66">''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''</ref>}}
'''Spar''', born '''Alpha-Ø2''' and later known as '''Mandalore the Resurrector''', was a rogue [[Alpha-class Advanced Recon Commando]] who rebuilt the [[Mandalorian Supercommando]]s and fought for the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Alpha-Ø2 was [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] from the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] for the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. An aberrant clone with more independence and free-thinking than his fellow [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], Alpha-Ø2 found after eight-and-a-half years that he had inherited the genetic memories of Jango Fett. With the aid of ''[[Cuy'val Dar]]'' [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]] [[Mij Gilamar]], Alpha-Ø2 made his escape off Kamino in the hold of [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']], taking the name "Spar" from what the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] trainers had called him.

Spar traveled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter before the Clone Wars. When war broke out and Jango Fett was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]], Spar realized that he needed to reform the Mandalorian Supercommandos and traveled to the planet [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]. After overcoming the pacifist [[New Mandalorians/Legends|movement]] of [[Duchess/Legends|Duchess]] [[Satine Kryze/Legends|Satine Kryze]], Spar became [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] and formed the [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]], recruiting two hundred police from Mandalore—including [[Fenn Shysa]] and [[Tobbi Dala]]—and a dozen former [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] members. He took the name "Mandalore the Resurrector" as a sign that he would honor the old traditions of the Mandalorians. Hating the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] for their killing of Mandalorians at the [[Battle of Galidraan]], Spar joined the war on the side of the Confederacy of Independent Systems.

The Protectors fought throughout the war at [[Null/Legends|Null]], [[New Bornalex/Legends|New Bornalex]], Kamino, [[New Holstice/Legends|New Holstice]], [[Zaadja/Legends|Zaadja]], and even Mandalore, where they seized the [[MandalMotors tower|headquarters]] of [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]] for Separatist use. Spar brought new matériel to the Mandalorian cause, including [[Pursuer-class enforcement ship|''Pursuer''-class enforcement ships]] and the [[BL-series Battle Legionnaire]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]]. Their final mission ended in betrayal when [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] ordered the Protectors to capture [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] on [[Norval II]]—a [[Mission to Norval II|mission]] that proved to be a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] ambush. Only three survivors walked away from the battle: Shysa, Dala, and a shell-shocked Spar. Spar left the post of Mandalore but remained on the planet Mandalore for some months longer. During his stay, he joined with the colleagues of [[Kal Skirata]] to retrieve information that could help cure the rapid aging of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]]. He eventually disappeared into the [[Extrictarium Nebula/Legends|Extrictarium Nebula]], where, years later, [[Ailyn Vel]]—the daughter of [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]—mistook him for her father and killed him.

==Biography==
===Life on Kamino===
{{Quote|Spar saw what was coming and thought he would be better off taking his chances outside. And once we knew he was gone—well, quite a few of us started thinking.|Sull|<ref name="TC" />}}
Alpha-Ø2 was [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] from the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] by the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] cloners on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], one of the first [[Alpha-class Advanced Recon Commando|test class]] of [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|Advanced Recon Commando]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] made as part of the order for the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. While the Kaminoans instilled uniformity in their clones, limiting the choices and individuality available to them, Alpha-Ø2 was a case of a one percent failure rate in the Kaminoans' product. Of the hundred ARC [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] in his class, he alone was independent and free-thinking, rejecting the anonymity enforced on him,<ref name="GATORW" /> and internalizing no loyalty to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="O66" /> Like all aberrant clones—which appeared in 3.5 percent of the general clone population—Alpha-Ø2 was put through reconditioning. The process did not take, but Alpha-Ø2 seemed to blend in with the other clones, and his peculiarity was not noticed.<ref name="HOTM" /> The Kaminoan handlers came to call him "Spar."<ref name="GATORW" /> Alpha-Ø2 developed a friendship with [[Mij Gilamar]], one of the Mandalorian ''[[Cuy'val Dar]]'' trainers working as a [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]].<ref name="O66" /> Eight-and-a-half years after his cloning, when he was biologically seventeen years of age, Alpha-Ø2 heard a mention of Jango Fett's exploits, which triggered a memory in him—not of his own life, but of Fett's. Fett's memories returned to Alpha-Ø2 in a rush; and, knowing that they would be reconditioned out of him, he prepared to make his escape from Kamino.<ref name="HOTM" /> Alpha-Ø2 began faking insanity and headaches to meet with Gilamar. Gilamar eventually called in a favor with Jango Fett to smuggle Alpha-Ø2 off Kamino in the hold of Fett's [[Starship/Legends|ship]], [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']]. Alpha-Ø2's escape made him the first deserter from the Grand [[Army/Legends|Army]].<ref name="O66" />

[[File:MandalorianProtectors-GAW.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The Mandalorian Protectors]]
Upon leaving Kamino, Alpha-Ø2 decided to take the name "Spar" as his own. He took to working odd jobs around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], particularly in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]],<ref name="GATORW" /> as he attempted to sort through his mental state.<ref name="HOTM" /> The man mostly took work as a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter, specializing in live captures.<ref name="O66" /> When Jango Fett was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|opening battle]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Spar concluded that his purpose was to rebuild the [[Mandalorian Supercommando]]s. Spar traveled to the planet [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], where he called for aid from the local police to form his new [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]]. Two hundred police came to him; among them were two constables of Mandalore named [[Fenn Shysa]] and [[Tobbi Dala]], as well as twelve former members of the splinter Mandalorian faction, the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]]. A rumor spread across Mandalore that Spar was the son of Jango Fett who had come to rebuild the Mandalorians.<ref name="HOTM" /> He was opposed in this by [[Duchess/Legends|Duchess]] [[Satine Kryze/Legends|Satine Kryze]], leader of the pacifist [[New Mandalorians/Legends|New Mandalorians]], who wished Mandalore to remain neutral in the Clone Wars. Spar won out over her faction due to her death in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], however, and assumed the position of [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]], leader of the Mandalorians. He took the title "Mandalore the Resurrector" as a sign that the old traditions of the Mandalorians would hold dominant.<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Rumors existed that Shysa was the true power behind Spar, but most recognized Shysa as Spar's most loyal follower.<ref name="GAW" />

===Mandalore===
[[File:Mandaloriansclonewars.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Spar (middle), Shysa (left), and Dala (right) in The Clone Wars]]
Spar, bearing a desire for order and a hatred of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] for its part in killing the Mandalorians at the [[Battle of Galidraan]], joined the Protectors to the cause of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].<ref name="GAW" /> The Mandalore put the [[Pursuer-class enforcement ship|''Pursuer''-class enforcement ship]] into use among his unit.<ref name="HOTM" /> Recalling how [[Mandallian Giant]]s had been used by the Mandalorians on the front lines of the [[New Sith Wars]], as well as Fenn Shysa's encounter with the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[C-3PX]], Spar also had the droid foundries of the Separatists produce a limited run of one thousand [[BL-series Battle Legionnaire]] droids.<ref name="NEGTD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> Spar and his Protectors fought the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and Republic around the galaxy, including at [[New Holstice/Legends|New Holstice]], [[Null/Legends|Null]], and [[Zaadja/Legends|Zaadja]].<ref name="GAW" /> Supercommando [[Squad/Legends|squads]] engaged in covert missions on behalf of the Separatists. Near the climax of the war, Spar and the Protectors helped the government of Mandalore capture the [[MandalMotors tower|facilities]] of [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]] in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]] for Separatist use. When a prominent Separatist [[Commander/Legends|commander]] was killed, the Mandalorians and their Battle Legionnaire droids retaliated at [[New Bornalex/Legends|New Bornalex]] and Kamino.<ref name="HOTM" /> The Republic sent [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] after Spar, but they failed to kill him.<ref name="TC" />

Near the end of the war, secret Separatist leader [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] instructed the Mandalorian government to [[Mission to Norval II|dispatch]] Spar and his supercommandos to [[Norval II]] to capture [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]]. Sidious betrayed them, however, and the mission sent the group into a Republic ambush. All but three of the Protectors were killed—Spar, Shysa, and Dala alone survived. The shell-shocked Spar left his post of Mandalore.<ref name="HOTM" /> He remained on the planet Mandalore for some time after, and Shysa made repeated unsuccessful attempts to get the ex-Mandalore to resume his post.<ref name="O66" />

===After the Protectors===
{{Quote|I bet they wish they'd asked a few more questions before accepting command of a slave army now.|Spar, reacting to Order 66|<ref name="O66" />}}
In the last half-year of the war, Spar met the discharged-dead clone, [[Fi Skirata|Fi]] in the city of [[Enceri]]; in the following days, the two met again in the ''[[Oyu'baat]]'' [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcafe]] in Keldabe, where Spar was joined by Shysa and [[Sull]], another ARC trooper deserter. Hoping a Fett could hold together the people of Mandalore, Shysa tried to convince Spar once again to return to his role. Spar allowed Shysa to float the idea that he was considering a return, but did not commit; he ultimately would refuse the position. The meeting ended with the arrival of ex-Jedi [[Bardan Jusik]]; Sull bore a grudge against Jedi and started an altercation, which Spar attempted to break up. Spar himself still held resentment for Jusik as a Jedi, however, and the two ARC troopers confronted Jusik and Fi as they were preparing to leave for [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Jusik put himself at their mercy, and after learning that he bore no more allegiance to the Jedi, Spar backed down, with Sull following suit. Having heard rumors that Jusik's friend, [[Kal Skirata]], was in pursuit of a cure for the clones' rapid aging and shortened lifespan, Spar verified the story with Jusik. Jusik confirmed it, and Spar and Sull joined the other two to head to Coruscant.<ref name="O66" />

Spar reunited with Gilimar and met with Skirata a month later on Coruscant. The ex-Mandalore agreed to join with Skirata in exchange for a share of whatever treatment they found. Skirata informed them of [[Ovolot Qail Uthan]], a Separatist [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] who had worked on the clone genome and could help them; at the time, she was in the Republic's custody. He also wanted to free his daughter, [[Ruusaan Skirata|Ruusaan]], [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] in the [[Republic Detention Center]] in [[Pols Anaxes]]. It was to this mission that Spar was assigned, alongside Sull, Fi, and Jusik. After almost two months of preparation, the trio of clones infiltrated the center on [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]] disguised in [[Clone trooper armor/Legends|clone trooper armor]], with Jusik as their supposed [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]]. They claimed to the camp commander that Jusik was there to identify Ruusaan, presenting false credentials to move her to Coruscant. Upon removing Ruusaan from her cell, the girl proved recalcitrant; Spar had to restrain her until Jusik administered a [[Force Stasis/Legends|Force Stun]]. They were able to get her to their [[Starship/Legends|spacecraft]] and escape the planet. They rendezvoused with Skirata's adopted son, [[Ordo Skirata|Ordo]], on Coruscant, right in the middle of the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]]. Jusik was able to get Spar offworld in his [[Aggressor assault fighter/Legends|Aggressor assault fighter]], along with Sull; Ruusaan Skirata; ARC trooper [[Mereel Skirata|Mereel]]; Uthan; and Jango Fett's sister, [[Arla Fett|Arla]]—the latter two of whom had been sprung from custody on Coruscant in the days prior.<ref name="O66" />

With Spar refusing to return, Fenn Shysa succeeded him as Mandalore.<ref name="O66" /> Spar went to Skirata's stronghold at [[Kyrimorut/Legends|Kyrimorut]] a few months later to be immunized by Uthan against the [[FG36]] virus, a [[Bioweapon/Legends|bioweapon]] designed by Uthan and now in use by the new [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. While there, Spar learned of Uthan's progress on her cure for rapid aging and volunteered to trial anything that she developed.<ref name="501st">''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''</ref> Spar eventually disappeared into the [[Extrictarium Nebula/Legends|Extrictarium Nebula]]. There, he succumbed to [[clone madness]] as his mind attempted to sort out the two sets of memories within it. Years later, [[Ailyn Vel]] found him within the nebula. By this point, Spar was unsure whether he was experiencing reality or hallucinating. Believing Spar to be her father, [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], Vel shot the man dead.<ref name="HOTM" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|Our chief officer, who'd become disenchanted with fightin' fer other people, set off on his own an' ended up the canniest bounty hunter in the known worlds. That, o' course, was Boba Fett.|Fenn Shysa, in actuality speaking of Spar|<ref name="SW68">[[Star Wars (1977) 68|''Star Wars'' (1977) 68]]</ref>}}
Ailyn Vel took Spar's [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]] and ARC trooper [[Kama/Legends|''kama'']], using it to masquerade as Boba Fett in her job as a bounty hunter.<ref name="HOTM" /> She was not the only one who mistook him as such, however. Because the Protectors had known him as the son of Jango Fett,<ref name="GAW" /> Fenn Shysa identified his commander in the Clone Wars as Boba Fett decades later.<ref name="SW68" />

==Personality and traits==
As a clone on Kamino, Alpha-Ø2 showed independence far beyond the norm for his fellow soldiers, and the uniform anonymity enforced in the Kaminoan facilities rankled his free-thinking personality.<ref name="GATORW" /> During his training, Alpha-Ø2 saw what was coming for the clone army, and it was this belief that partly drove him to escape.<ref name="TC" /> Fi, a deserter from the Grand Army, did not believe that Spar felt guilt over his own desertion; however, he suspected that the ARC trooper had some regret in his past. Though never a cheerful man,<ref name="O66" /> Spar was affected by the war—it removed something from him, leaving him shell-shocked and eventually bringing forth his descent into complete delusion and inability to distinguish reality.<ref name="HOTM" />

Spar was a perceptive and knowledgeable individual who kept track of information that passed through Mandalore. He was adept in stealth combat tactics and was able to throw off enemies trying to track his location.<ref name="O66" /> His training and the skills he inherited from Fett gave him an edge in survival in the wider galaxy.<ref name="GATORW" /> Unlike other clone troopers, Spar did not abide by rules of engagement, preferring to kill rather than to take prisoners.<ref name="O66" />

Spar put great stock in the old traditions of the Mandalorians.<ref name="Atlas" /> He deplored Fett for selling his genes to create the Grand Army; however, Spar was a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] himself, seeking payment for jobs he took. He held a continuing grudge against the Jedi, even resenting those who had left the Order. Spar took great satisfaction upon learning of the Jedi Order's demise at the hands of the clone army during [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], believing it a reflection on their willingness to lead the Grand Army.<ref name="O66" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|I do try to keep inventing new nouns down to a minimum, because I think the Star Wars universe is so vast that somebody has usually written about something you're planning to use. Need an abberant [sic] clone? Well, here's Spar from Geonosis and the Outer Rim.|Abel G. Peña|<ref>{{JCFCite|url=posts/26474668|board=Literature|thread=The Emperor's Pawns article (was: Gamer #5 stuff @ TOS!!!)|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2007-05-31|time=1:47 PM|archivedate=20200801195123}}</ref>}}
Spar was first created for the [[roleplaying game]] sourcebook ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''—by [[Daniel Wallace]], [[Jason Fry]], [[Craig Robert Carey]], [[Jeff Quick]], and [[Jeffrey Quinn]]—as a non-playable character for use in adventures. Spar's character description was brief, only detailing his biography up to his escape and ending with a note that Jango Fett might hunt down the rogue clone in the future. It was [[Abel G. Peña]] who [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] Spar in the [[2005]] ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' article [[The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors|''The History of the Mandalorians'']] to explain a decades-old reference that, with the release of the [[prequel trilogy]], was now a continuity error.

The [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel Star Wars]] comic [[Star Wars (1977) 68|''Star Wars'' (1977) 68]], published in [[1982]], told how the Mandalorian Protectors had fought in the Clone Wars. According to a story told by Fenn Shysa in the comic, Shysa and his friend, Tobbi Dala, had fought under Boba Fett, serving the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] to fight the Jedi. During this time, they had been ordered to capture [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]]. Most of these references became a problem with later revelations about the Clone Wars: Boba Fett was only ten to thirteen years old during the conflict, Leia was only born after their end, and the Emperor, Palpatine, had only been the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] at the time. The latter two inconsistencies were solved by having the Mandalorians serve Palpatine in his persona of Darth Sidious and having them capture Leia's mother, Padmé Amidala, instead. To solve the issue of Boba Fett's appearance, Peña had had the idea of Alpha-Ø2 in mind for some time. When ''Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds'' was released, Peña realized that Spar could be fit into the role that he had planned. His retcon made Spar the leader of the Mandalorians in the time of the Clone Wars, and due to a rumor that he was the "son of Jango Fett", characters such as Shysa would think it had been Boba leading them. Peña also created Spar's clone name, Alpha-Ø2. The use of the "Ø" was an instance of image association, a visual sign that set Spar apart and signaled that he was defective in some way.<ref name="Abel Spar name">{{JCFCite|url=posts/18306316|board=Literature|thread="Insider #80 to feature Mandalorian article, Yo Joe!"|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2005-02-06|time=2:51 AM|archiveurl=https://archive.is/GccqY}}</ref> Due to a layout error in the printing of the article, Spar's image was mistakenly placed over a heading of Jango and Boba Fett. The large scar visible on Spar's face originated in an earlier draft of the article's description of Spar.<ref name="Abel Spar image">{{JCFCite|url=posts/18101153|board=Literature|thread="Insider #80 to feature Mandalorian article, Yo Joe!"|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2005-01-18|time=11:32 PM|archiveurl=https://archive.is/aU7nn}}</ref> The article did not give a timeframe for Spar's death; Peña estimates that it occurred between [[7 ABY/Legends|7]] and [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], after the [[Star Wars: Boba Fett: Death, Lies, & Treachery|''Boba Fett: Death, Lies, & Treachery'']] trade paperback.<ref name="Abel Spar death">{{JCFCite|url=posts/18223784|board=Literature|thread="Insider #80 to feature Mandalorian article, Yo Joe!"|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2005-01-30|time=3:49 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801144224/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/insider-80-to-feature-mandalorian-article-yo-joe.17497617/page-36}}</ref>

New errors arrived with the release of the novel ''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' by [[Karen Traviss]]. While earlier works had established Fenn Shysa as a follower of Spar who was mistaken about his identity, ''Revelation'' stated that Shysa was the leader behind Spar, using Spar as a figurehead Mandalore and claiming that he was Boba. Traviss's next novel, ''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'', radically altered Spar's story further. In the novel, Spar is said to have refused Shysa's request to be Mandalore. He never becomes Mandalore and never joins the Separatists; the novel ends with Shysa becoming what is said to be the first Mandalore since Jango Fett. This new version of events, however, has been an anomaly; later sources such as ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', and ''[[Galaxy at War]]'' confirm that Spar was Mandalore, with the latter source specifically addressing the Shysa aspect as a rumor. Other errors have since occurred—the sequel to ''Order 66'', ''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'', states Spar received inoculations for Separatist viruses, a statement made difficult by the fact that Spar deserted the Grand Army of the Republic before the Separatists ever went to war against the Republic.
